meta.title: Compact Bulky Waste Processing Layout Case Study | WEI LAN
meta.description: Review the WEI LAN compact bulky waste processing layout reference, including case description, Lion One process modules, site details, buyer background, and video reference.

bodyHtml:
Bulky Waste Case Detail

Compact bulky waste processing layout

Reference for compact bulky waste preprocessing where the buyer needs receiving, size reduction, separation, and baled output in one workflow.

Reference Timeline

From oversized waste handling to compact preprocessing

The case is ordered by buyer evaluation flow: background, cooperation unit, constraints, solution, and before/after comparison.

01

Project background

Oversized mixed waste needed preprocessing before disposal

The layout handles bulky waste and light mixed waste by reducing feed size, separating recoverable fractions, and preparing residue for downstream handling.

Material family
2 streamsbulky and light mixed waste
Target route
1 workflowreceive to baled output
02

Cooperation unit

Anonymized sanitation service operator

The cooperation unit planned to reduce bulky waste transport volume and recover recyclable fractions before final disposal or downstream transfer.

Operator type
1 sanitation teamtransfer-station operation
Buyer concern
2 goalsvolume reduction and recovery
03

Real constraints

The footprint had to work inside transfer-station logistics

The site needed a receiving bay, bulky material staging, equipment lane, recovered fraction storage, and truck loading area within a compact operating footprint.

Site zones
5 zonesbay to loading area
Layout type
compactsingle workflow path
04

Solution

Lion One process modules with separation and baling interface

The process scope combines crushing, screening, air separation, conveying, optional magnetic separation, baling interface, and remote-control support.

Process modules
4 modulescrush / screen / air / convey
Interfaces
2 interfacesbaling and remote control
05

Before / after comparison

The reference shows a more controllable bulky waste route

BeforeOversized waste moved as loose mixed material

Transport and staging pressure stayed high before size reduction and recovery checks.

AfterReduced, separated, and ready for downstream handling

The reference video shows feeding, crushing, air separation, discharge, and the baling interface in one workflow.

Video scope
5 stepsfeed to baling interface
Request Reference Package Back to Case Studies
